feat(desktop): add OpenASE desktop shell v1 (#583)

* feat(desktop): add OpenASE desktop shell v1

Summary:
- add a dedicated Electron desktop host with service lifecycle control,
  local shell views, packaging scripts, and desktop tests
- wire desktop build, validate, and packaging targets into the repo
  Makefile and CI workflow, and document the desktop runtime and
  PostgreSQL v1 strategy in English and Chinese docs
- refresh local API and ephemeral chat black-box scripts so desktop
  validation can reuse stable service smoke coverage, including a
  deterministic fake-codex mode for chat smoke tests

Rationale:
- deliver the desktop shell without rewriting the Go backend, web UI,
  or PostgreSQL data model
- keep the desktop integration isolated from existing service layers
  while still making CI and local validation workflow-aligned
- reduce environment-specific drift in the smoke scripts so the new
  desktop flow can be validated reliably on local machines

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH make web-validate
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH make desktop-validate

- python3 scripts/dev/test_ephemeral_chat_blackbox.py --base-url http://127.0.0.1:19836 --provider-mode fake-codex --timeout-seconds 90

docs(docs): align publishing flow with DocsQuartz deployments (#2)

Document the two-repository publishing pipeline and add the source-repo
trigger that tells DocsQuartz to rebuild from docs/main. This keeps docs
as the source of truth while making the operational setup explicit for
GitHub Pages, secrets, and custom-domain wiring.

Constraint: docs must remain the source of truth with no mirrored content commits

Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean

Tested: YAML parse for docs workflow; Prettier check for new docs files; end-to-end Quartz temp build using docs content

commit.message:
Merge PR #2: Julia MCWF migration

### Motivation

- Port the MATLAB Monte Carlo wavefunction (MCWF / MC quantum jumps) implementation to Julia using `QuantumOptics.jl` and provide a high-performance, thread-parallel core optimized for production runs and comparisons with the MATLAB reference.
- Provide reproducible project configuration and developer tooling (instantiation, benchmark, MATLAB-compatibility verification, and a Jupyter entry notebook) to ease validation and server-scale parameter scans.

### Description

- Add a new Julia package structure with `Project.toml` and `Manifest.toml` and a top-level module `src/QuantumMC.jl` that exposes the `MCWFOptimized` submodule.
- Implement the MCWF core in `src/MCWFOptimized.jl` including `MCWFParams`/`RunConfig` types, per-thread `TrajCache`, `run_trajectory!` single-trajectory logic, and `run_ensemble` threaded execution with thread-local RNGs and caches.
- Fix numerical/physics issues versus the original port: use the full kinetic energy expression including the cross term, handle possibly-negative discriminants by converting to `ComplexF64` in `_ep32`, and perform in-place, allocation-free operations (`mul!`, `copyto!`, preallocated buffers) to reduce GC pressure.
- Add documentation, usage and operational guides in `README.md` and `docs/` (Chinese), a Jupyter notebook `notebooks/QuantumMC_入口.ipynb`, and utility scripts `scripts/verify_against_matlab.jl` and `scripts/benchmark.jl` for correctness verification against a MATLAB `.mat` reference and for benchmarking (including optional MATLAB `parfor` speedup comparison).

### Testing

- This PR does not include CI test runs and no automated tests were executed as part of the push.
- Added verification and benchmark helpers: run `scripts/verify_against_matlab.jl` (requires a `matlab_reference.mat` with `probability_ref`) for correctness and `scripts/benchmark.jl` for performance measurement; these scripts are intended for local/CI execution.
- Package instantiation is supported via `julia --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()'` to prepare dependencies before running the verification or benchmark scripts.
- No test failures were reported because no automated tests were run in this PR.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add keyboard and shell scaffolding

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Make the blog feel like one coherent publication

This change turns the recent Pretext enhancement work into a cohesive
site-wide visual system instead of a set of isolated page tweaks.
It vendors the Pretext runtime for progressive text layout on safe
plain-text blocks, updates the templates to expose those targets,
and redesigns home, post, archive, graph, and sidebar surfaces around
one editorial paper-plus-glass language.

I also cleaned the new CSS before landing it by pulling repeated hero
chip rules into shared styles and trimming page-specific duplication,
so the current look is easier to extend without reintroducing visual
branching.

Constraint: Keep the static Rust/Askama architecture and existing information layout
Constraint: Must preserve rich embeds, math, diagrams, and other non-plain-text content
Rejected: Full template/layout rewrite | too broad for the current push-first delivery
Rejected: Re-render all article HTML through Pretext | unsafe for nested rich markup
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Extend future polish through the shared panel/chip system before adding page-only style dialects
Tested: cargo test
Tested: cargo run -p blog-build -- --site-url https://example.test --title 'Test Garden' --description 'Test description'
Tested: node --check static/assets/pretext-runtime.mjs and vendored Pretext modules
Not-tested: Cross-browser manual QA beyond local screenshot checks
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add classification and Drive sync toolkit

Expand the repository beyond the tl;dv exporter by adding project classification, Drive archive publishing, and project-folder synchronization workflows. Document the full pipeline, provide a reusable rules template, and sanitize the public-facing defaults so the package can be shared without internal project names or folder IDs.

refactor(notification): align event wiring and toggles (#579)

Summary:
- align the notification catalog with the event sources that are actually
  wired through the bus and publish default grouped metadata in the API
- subscribe the engine to real ticket, agent, and activity topics so
  rule-driven delivery works for every supported notification event
- add a minimal grouped event toggle UI backed by notification rule CRUD
  plus backend and frontend regression coverage for catalog, wiring, and
  save/echo flows

Rationale:
- the previous catalog advertised events that never fired, which let the
  API and UI drift away from the backend contract
- reusing rule CRUD for toggles keeps this ticket focused on stable
  behavior instead of introducing a second notification config surface
- the added coverage locks catalog exposure, event routing, and grouped
  toggle behavior together to prevent future regressions

commit.message:
fix(ticket-detail): replay queued runtime drawer refreshes

Summary:
- queue ticket drawer timeline and reference refreshes before the
  initial loading guard short-circuits runtime events
- flush any queued refreshes after the active drawer load finishes so
  loading windows do not drop runtime state updates
- add regression tests for agent.ready/agent.executing drawer refreshes
  and the initial loading replay path

Rationale:
- runtime state changes such as running should be driven by backend
  events and reflected in the drawer as soon as the initial load settles
- the new tests lock the two reported regressions without broadening the
  implementation beyond the existing ticket-detail refresh flow

Tests:
- pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/features/ticket-detail/streams.test.ts src/lib/features/ticket-detail/drawer-state.svelte.test.ts src/lib/features/ticket-detail/drawer-state-loading.test.ts src/lib/features/board/components/board-page-runtime-and-drawer.test.ts
- corepack pnpm --dir web run ci

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
ASE-72: remove client-controlled audit actor writes (#577)

* refactor(httpapi): remove client-controlled audit actor writes

Summary:
- remove client-provided `created_by` and `edited_by` from ticket,
  workflow, project update, skill, and related HTTP request schemas
- route authoritative audit actors through server-derived principals,
  including project-conversation internal writeback and agent wrappers
- refresh OpenAPI, frontend generated API types, and CLI contracts to
  match the new write semantics

Rationale:
- make audit attribution authoritative on the server instead of letting
  clients override formal actor fields in request bodies
- keep special project-conversation attribution formats while moving
  them to server-generated context rather than compatibility fields
- prevent docs, generated types, and CLI entrypoints from preserving
  the old override model after the backend changes land

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/httpapi
  -run 'Test(PrepareProjectConversationActionBodyLeavesBodyUnchangedForExplicitAuditRoutes|PrepareProjectConversationActionBodyRejectsImplicitAuditRoutes|TicketRequestParserCoverage|TicketStatusAndWorkflowRequestParserCoverage|ProjectUpdateRequestParsersAndErrors|WorkflowRequestParsersCoverTrimmedAndInvalidInputs)$' -count=1
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/httpapi
  -run 'Test(TicketRoutesCRUDAndDependencies|TicketRoutesErrorMappingsAndInvalidPayloads|TicketRoutesCreateFirstTicketPerProjectAfterWorkflowCreate|TicketCommentRoutesCreateUpdateDelete|ProjectUpdateRoutesCRUDAndRevisions|WorkflowRoutesRoundTripExpandedPlatformAccessAllowed|WorkflowRoutesCRUDHarnessVersionsWithoutRepoSync|WorkflowRoutesPersistExplicitAuditActor|SkillRoutesImportBundleAndExposeFiles)$' -count=1
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/httpapi
  -run 'Test(AgentPlatformTicketRoutesRespectScopesAndBoundaries|AgentPlatformProjectUpdateRoutesRespectScopesAndBoundaries|AgentPlatformRouteErrorMappingsAndInvalidPayloads|BuildOpenAPIDocument|BuildOpenAPIJSONAndRoute|BuildOpenAPIDocumentRequestFieldsHaveDescriptions|OpenAPIProjectStreamsStayOnCanonicalBus)$' -count=1
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/cli
  -run 'Test(BuildSkillImportPayloadPackagesBundleDirectory|RootCLIAPICoverageMatchesOpenAPI|RootCLIBodyContracts|PlatformTicketUpdateHelpMentionsEnvFallbackAndUUIDSemantics|PlatformTicketCommentUpdateHelpMentionsBodyRules|TypedAPILeafCommandsProvideLongHelp|RootLeafCommandsProvideLongHelp|PlatformLeafCommandsProvideStructuredHelp)$' -count=1
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go run ./cmd/openase ticket update --help
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go run ./cmd/openase ticket comment create --help
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go run ./cmd/openase skill import --help
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go run ./cmd/openase openapi generate --output api/openapi.json
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH pnpm dlx openapi-typescript@7.13.0 api/openapi.json -o web/src/lib/api/generated/openapi.d.ts
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go run ./cmd/openase openapi cli-contract --output internal/cli/testdata/openapi_cli_contract.json

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(api): refresh generated openapi types

Summary:
- regenerate `web/src/lib/api/generated/openapi.d.ts` after merging the
  ASE-72 branch with latest `origin/main`
- align the frontend OpenAPI types with the current merged API document
  so `make openapi-check-ci` passes cleanly

Rationale:
- the CI gate regenerated the frontend contract and surfaced a real
  post-merge drift in the checked-in generated types
- keeping the generated frontend contract in sync avoids shipping a
  branch that passes backend tests but fails repository API checks

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh
  (initial run refreshed `web/src/lib/api/generated/openapi.d.ts` and
  failed on the resulting diff; rerun after this commit)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(httpapi): clear audit attribution lint blockers

Summary:
- replace agent platform wrapper struct literals with direct request type
  conversions to satisfy staticcheck without changing behavior
- remove dead project conversation action execution helpers that were left
  behind after the server-side audit actor refactor
- keep the server-authoritative attribution changes green under the repo CI
  gate after the branch sync with `origin/main`

Rationale:
- the audit attribution refactor was functionally complete, but the branch
  still failed lint and could not progress to review
- the direct conversions preserve the Parse, Not Validate request flow while
  avoiding redundant field-by-field copies
- removing unreachable helpers keeps chat API code aligned with the active
  project conversation writeback path and avoids misleading future readers

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor: remove machine transport legacy compatibility (#575)

* refactor(machines): remove legacy transport compatibility

Summary:
- remove `ssh_compat` and legacy transport aliases from machine
  domain parsing, HTTP API contracts, OpenAPI output, and frontend
  machine flows
- add a one-time migration that rewrites stored legacy `ssh`
  machine records to websocket listener semantics and update tests,
  mocks, fixtures, and docs to match
- tighten orchestrator, transport, and scheduler coverage so
  persisted legacy records are rejected or normalized internally
  without surfacing legacy semantics to clients

Rationale:
- machine transport now has a single supported remote execution
  model based on websocket reachability plus helper-only SSH access
- removing the compatibility aliases prevents new API and frontend
  code from depending on migration-era fields that should no longer
  be part of the product model

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/httpapi ./internal/infra/machinetransport
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/repo/catalog ./internal/repo/ticket ./internal/cli ./internal/machinechannel
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/orchestrator
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH pnpm vitest run src/lib/features/machines/model.test.ts src/lib/features/machines/machine-setup.test.ts src/lib/features/machines/components/machines-page.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(web): align machine transport ci validation

Summary:
- remove stale machine setup imports and lint violations that surfaced
  after syncing the branch with the latest mainline frontend checks
- update the machines e2e mock so the saved direct-connect runner keeps
  the required websocket listener endpoint during drawer save flows

Rationale:
- the publish gate must pass against the merged branch state, including
  the stricter frontend lint and Playwright expectations from main
- keeping the e2e fixture on supported transport semantics prevents the
  regression suite from editing an invalid legacy-style machine record

Tests:
- corepack pnpm --dir web run ci

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* style(go): apply transport cleanup formatting

Summary:
- run gofmt on the machine transport domain and HTTP request parsing
  files touched by the legacy cleanup
- keep the branch compliant with the repo fmt-check that runs inside the
  CI gate after syncing with the latest main branch

Rationale:
- the publish gate must pass on the merged branch state, and fmt-check is
  part of the required workflow-aligned validation for this ticket

Tests:
- OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT=$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(tests): cover legacy transport edge cases

Summary:
- make the project conversation SSH-resume tests declare the internal SSH
  helper transport explicitly instead of relying on removed legacy defaults
- extend the machine transport semantics tests to cover stored connection
  parsing and the remaining semantic-mode matrix branches

Rationale:
- the repo CI gate exercises broader packages than the initial targeted
  validation, and these tests still depended on implicit legacy transport
  behavior after the cleanup
- the additional domain coverage restores the required 100.0% domain/core
  threshold enforced by the backend coverage gate

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test -count=1 -run 'TestProjectConversationStartTurnResumesClaudeSessionOverSSHWithoutRecoveryPrompt|TestProjectConversationRespondInterruptRestoresCodexSessionOverSSHWhenRuntimeMissing|TestProjectConversationRespondInterruptResumesClaudeSessionOverSSHAndContinuesInterruptedTurn' ./internal/chat
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH bash <<'EOF2'
  set -euo pipefail
  domain_coverpkg=$(go list ./internal/domain/... ./internal/types/... | paste -sd, -)
  go test -count=1 -covermode=atomic -coverpkg="$domain_coverpkg" -coverprofile=/tmp/ase68-domain.out ./internal/domain/... ./internal/types/... >/tmp/ase68-domain.log
  go tool cover -func=/tmp/ase68-domain.out | tail -n 1
  EOF2

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(ci): rerun backend checks

* chore(ci): rerun frontend checks

commit.message:
fix(push): explicit params to fix stale closure + remove Device.isDevice guard (#1095)

* fix(push): pass explicit userId/merchantId to avoid stale closures

React Compiler memoization causes registerPush() closures to capture
stale user/merchant values. Pass them explicitly from the layout effects
where the values are known to be current.

Also removes Device.isDevice guard that falsely blocks real TestFlight
devices when expo-device is dynamically imported.

- Admin test: wait for render before negative assertion
- Admin hook: wrap AsyncStorage load in try/catch with void
- Storefront layout: use subscribed storeMerchantId instead of
  getState(), remove stale eslint-disable comment

commit.message:
Work (#9)

* Add contacts import (Google Sheets/YClients), DB migrations, and clients/staff/settings UI/hooks updates

### Motivation
- Provide a robust path to import contacts from either YClients or Google Sheets and persist import metadata in org settings.
- Make clients UI more feature-rich (age/discount filters, wider table with left-accent scrollbar) and keep staff UI in sync with real masters data from the DB.
- Ensure database schema supports YClients-compatible client fields and automatic linking between user records and clients when YClients IDs appear.

### Description
- Added an API endpoint `app/api/clients/import/route.ts` that normalizes rows from `yclients` or `google_sheets`, upserts/inserts into `clients`, and writes import metadata into `org_settings`.
- Implemented `lib/imports/normalizeGoogleSheetsClient.ts` to adapt Google Sheets rows into the clients schema and added `normalizeYclientsRow` logic in the import route.
- Introduced DB migrations and helper SQL: `add_contacts_import_source.sql`, `add_yclients_clients_columns.sql`, `link_users_to_clients_on_yc_id.sql`, seed and reset updates and a seeds file `seed_test_masters.sql` to populate masters for staff cards.
- Updated frontend: extended clients filters and table (`app/clients/page.tsx`) with age/discount filters, redesigned table layout and local left-accent scrollbar (`app/globals.css`), added settings UI to pick and save import source (`app/settings/page.tsx`), and disabled/marked in-development auto-systems in `app/system/page.tsx`.
- Refactored data hooks: `lib/hooks/useClients.ts` now reads from `clients` and normalizes new fields (discount, channels, birthday, etc.), and added a new `lib/hooks/useYclientsMasters.ts` hook to fetch/subscribe to `masters` for the staff UI; `app/staff/page.tsx` updated to consume masters and render realistic KPIs and cards.

### Testing
- Ran TypeScript/Next.js build (`next build`) and static type-checking locally and the build completed without type errors.
- Ran linter/format checks (ESLint) and no lint errors were reported for modified files.
- Performed basic automated smoke checks by running the dev server and loading `Clients`, `Staff`, and `Settings` pages to ensure hooks and new components render without runtime errors (no automated unit tests were changed or present for these new features).

* Store messenger user IDs on clients during yc_id auto-link

* Add reverse sync from clients to messenger user tables

---------

commit.message:
fix: stabilize Project AI conversation ownership across origins (#571)

* fix(chat): stabilize project conversation ownership

Summary:
- route persistent project conversation APIs through a stable owner
  resolver that uses the authenticated human in OIDC mode and the
  server-defined `local-user:default` principal when auth is disabled
- normalize legacy non-OIDC conversation owners during list/get/watch so
  existing project conversations remain visible after switching frontend
  ports or losing browser-local state
- stop sending `X-OpenASE-Chat-User` on persistent project conversation
  requests, document the auth-disabled fallback, and refresh the
  controller restore tests around mux streaming

Rationale:
- project conversation ownership must come from the server instead of a
  browser-local random id, otherwise port changes create a new owner
  namespace and break restore/list semantics
- auth-disabled mode is a local-instance fallback, so a stable shared
  principal is safer and more explainable than coupling persistence to
  `localStorage`

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/httpapi ./internal/chat ./internal/repo/chatconversation
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/lib/api/chat.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller-restore.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller-restore-live.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(web): trim human auth settings budget

Summary:
- inline the RBAC binding action callbacks in the human auth settings
  component
- remove helper-only lines so the file stays below the enforced hard
  budget after the auth-mode messaging update

Rationale:
- the merge-time CI gate failed on the file budget check after the
  persistent conversation ownership copy landed
- this keeps the UX change while preserving the repo's frontend size
  guardrails

Tests:
- OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT=$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest \
  OPENASE_GO_TEST_PROGRESS_MODE=json \
  PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH \
  .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

ASE-71: unify AI ownership under server-defined principals (#573)

* fix(chat): use server-defined ai principals

Summary:
- resolve human chat, project conversation, and skill refinement owners
  from a server-defined principal helper instead of request headers
- issue a stable `openase_ai_principal` cookie in auth-disabled mode and
  remove browser-local owner id generation from the web chat client
- update HTTP, frontend, and blackbox tests plus docs to reflect the new
  server-authored ownership contract

Rationale:
- AI session ownership must stay consistent across project chat,
  conversations, and refinement flows without trusting browser-local ids
- OIDC and non-OIDC modes now share one explainable ownership entrypoint:
  human principal when authenticated, browser-session cookie otherwise
- removing `X-OpenASE-Chat-User` from the client prevents the frontend from
  inventing authoritative owner identities

Tests:
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH OPENASE_GO_TEST_PROGRESS_MODE=json go test ./internal/httpapi ./internal/chat
- python3 -m py_compile scripts/dev/test_ephemeral_chat_blackbox.py
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/api/chat.test.ts
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/features/workflows/components/harness-ai-sidebar-streaming.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(auth): sync zh ai principal ownership note

Summary:
- update the Chinese human-auth RBAC doc to describe the same
  server-defined AI principal model as the English doc
- document the auth-disabled `openase_ai_principal` cookie and the
  removal of `X-OpenASE-Chat-User` as an authoritative owner input

Rationale:
- ASE-71 requires docs and contracts to converge on one explainable AI
  ownership model across OIDC and non-OIDC browser flows
- leaving the zh guide on the old browser-local wording would keep the
  ticket's documentation scope incomplete

Tests:
- not run (docs-only follow-up to align translated guidance)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Bump version to 1.1.0 with multilingual help support

  window-title selectors (Settings — Aire, Welcome to Aire)

commit.message:
feat: generate multi-repo workspace instruction hubs (#566)

* feat(orchestrator): generate multi-repo instruction hubs

Summary:
- add multi-repo workspace-root instruction hub generation in the
  ticket workspace provisioner for Codex and Claude adapters
- discover canonical AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files deterministically
  while pruning tool-owned directories
- cover single-repo no-op behavior plus local and websocket multi-repo
  materialization in orchestrator tests

Rationale:
- multi-repo ticket runs start from the ticket workspace root, so repo-
  local instructions are otherwise easy for the active CLI to miss
- keeping the helper in the orchestration/materialization layer lets the
  same behavior work across local and remote workspace preparation paths

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/orchestrator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(orchestrator): satisfy instruction hub lint gate

Summary:
- annotate safe workspace file reads in the new instruction-hub paths and
  launcher coverage tests
- preallocate the remote discovery command slice to satisfy the lint gate

Rationale:
- the repo CI gate runs gosec and prealloc checks, so the new helper needs
  explicit safe-path annotations for orchestrator-managed files

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/orchestrator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(orchestrator): use root-scoped hub reads

Summary:
- switch local instruction-hub discovery to read through `os.DirFS`
  instead of direct callback paths from `filepath.WalkDir`

Rationale:
- the repo CI gate enforces gosec's root-scoped filesystem guidance for
  walk callbacks, so the helper needs race-safe repo-root reads

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/orchestrator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

commit.message:
perf(flow-core): tighten pipeline and task-group hot paths

Summary:
- make RuntimeJoinScope and TaskGroup state lazy, and route TaskGroup children through a lighter dedicated path
- cache graph pending state and add a small inline-graph fast path to cut executor setup overhead
- reduce pipeline serial-stage hot-path overhead and add regression coverage for token accounting and ordering

Rationale:
- pipeline and recursive task orchestration were still the biggest validated hot spots in the latest comparison runs
- isolating the verified flow-core changes now keeps the next pipeline-focused round easier to measure and revert if needed

Validation:
- cargo test -p flow-core
- cargo run --release -p benchmarks --bin taskflow_compare -- --threads 4 --rounds 3 --cases fibonacci --output-dir benchmarks/reports/taskflow_compare/task_group_batch_only_fibonacci_20260406
- cargo run --release -p benchmarks --bin taskflow_compare -- --threads 4 --rounds 3 --cases data_pipeline --output-dir benchmarks/reports/taskflow_compare/pipeline_after_runtime_batch_revert_data_only_20260406

commit.message:
Work (#8)

* Integrate YClients data, enhance clients table & filters, add masters hook and DB migrations

### Motivation
- Migrate client handling toward YClients-compatible schema and enrich client filtering and table presentation for better audience selection.
- Replace mocked masters data with real `masters` rows and surface master KPIs in the Staff page.
- Improve UX for wide clients table with a persistent header, local accent scrollbar and scroll reset behavior.

### Description
- Add new numeric filters and columns to clients: `minAge`, `maxAge`, `minDiscount`, `maxDiscount`, display `discount` and `birthday` in the clients table, and restructure the table into a horizontally scrollable container with a sticky header in `app/clients/page.tsx`.
- Update client normalization and source table in `lib/hooks/useClients.ts` to map more YClients-like fields, add `discount` to the `Client` type, adapt channel mapping, and change Supabase queries/subscription from `clients_tg` to `clients`.
- Introduce `useYclientsMasters` hook (`lib/hooks/useYclientsMasters.ts`) to fetch/normalize `masters` from Supabase and update `app/staff/page.tsx` to consume this hook (replace mock data, compute totals, cards and KPI rows from live data).
- Add UI polish and behavior: left-accent local scrollbar styles in `app/globals.css`, `clientsTableScrollRef` scroll-reset effect, and segment -> status mapping for table badges.
- Add DB migration/seed SQL: `supabase/add_yclients_clients_columns.sql` to add needed YClients-compatible columns to `public.clients`, and `supabase/seed_test_masters.sql` to seed sample masters safely.
- Mark certain automation systems as "in development" and disable controls for them in `app/system/page.tsx` via `IN_DEVELOPMENT_SYSTEM_CODES`.

### Testing
- Ran TypeScript type-check (`tsc --noEmit`) and a local project build verification (`next build`) to validate typings and bundling, both completed without errors.
- No new unit tests were added in this change set and existing test suites were not modified.

* Add DB sync from messenger users to clients by yc_id

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(csr): add separate staff login flow (#24)

Summary:
- replace the landing page greeting form with a CSR-aware portal entry
- add dedicated CSR login and dashboard routes with isolated session storage
- cover portal entry, login, redirect, and logout behavior with node tests

Rationale:
- CSR authentication needs its own routes and session boundary before
  customer authentication is introduced
- the protected dashboard path must deny unauthenticated access and
  return authenticated staff directly to their queue entry point

Tests:
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
ASE-57: add first-class agent interrupt control (#561)

* feat(agent): add first-class runtime interrupt control

Summary:
- add explicit agent interrupt semantics across catalog state, run
  status, activity events, ticket diagnosis, HTTP API, CLI, and
  OpenAPI artifacts
- reconcile interrupt requests in the orchestrator so active runs stop
  through the platform control plane and persist interrupted terminal
  state instead of generic termination
- expose interrupt controls in Agents UI and Project AI with wording
  that keeps agent interrupt distinct from Project AI runtime close,
  and add focused frontend coverage

Rationale:
- the product already claimed interrupt support, but the platform only
  implemented pause and resume, which left runtime control, auditing,
  and UI semantics inconsistent
- first-class interrupt state and scope boundaries are needed so
  operators and Project AI can stop platform agents safely without
  conflating agent runs with conversation runtime lifecycle

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/domain/catalog ./internal/service/catalog ./internal/ticket ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/httpapi ./internal/agentplatform
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/cli ./internal/httpapi
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go run ./cmd/openase openapi generate --output api/openapi.json
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go run ./cmd/openase openapi cli-contract --output internal/cli/testdata/openapi_cli_contract.json
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH pnpm --dir web check
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH pnpm --dir web build
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH pnpm --dir web exec vitest run src/lib/features/agents/components/agent-drawer.test.ts src/lib/features/agents/components/agents-page.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-focus.test.ts src/lib/features/ticket-detail/components/ticket-runtime-state-card.test.ts src/lib/features/ticket-detail/run-transcript-data.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(agent): fix interrupt gate follow-ups

Summary:
- update domain scope and catalog coverage tests for the new interrupt
  scope and runtime-control branches
- extract focused Project AI interrupt target derivation and trim agent
  UI files to satisfy frontend file budgets
- apply required formatting-only follow-ups from the CI-compatible gate

Rationale:
- the first publish gate exposed budget, formatting, and domain coverage
  drift that was orthogonal to the feature logic but still required for
  repo workflow alignment

commit.message:
refactor(workflow): stop materializing runtime harness files

Summary:
- stop writing workflow harness snapshots into .openase/harnesses during
  runtime materialization
- remove remote sync handling that copied harness files and instead clear
  legacy harness directories from workspaces
- update runtime snapshot tests to assert harness files stay absent while
  skill projection still works

Rationale:
- workflow harness content is already injected from the recorded runtime
  snapshot, so writing a duplicate file in the workspace is redundant
- removing the legacy materialization path reduces stale workspace state
  and matches the control-plane-owned harness model

Tests:
- make openapi-check-ci
- env OPENASE_BACKEND_TEST_GROUP_SIZE=8 make check
- make build
- make LINT_BASE_REV=73acc4c6fca3ffbf1297b6cfc427b8e13a0461d7 lint
- make lint-depguard
- make lint-architecture

docs(spec): align macOS launchd support narrative (#559)

Summary:

  macOS launchd as part of the current shipped contract
- align the implemented-scope bullets and F29 planning entries with
  the cross-platform managed-service behavior already documented

Rationale:

  public docs were updated for shipped macOS support
- keeping the internal spec aligned with README and docs avoids

commit.message:
refactor(workflow): use project control roots for chat and hooks

Summary:
- route workflow lifecycle hooks through per-project control roots under
  ~/.openase/projects instead of inferring a git repo from cwd
- derive default chat working directories from per-project control roots
  while keeping Project AI runtime workspaces unchanged
- update CLI and HTTP API tests to use explicit environment and hook path
  expectations for the new control-root semantics

Rationale:
- workflow startup should not depend on systemd or the current process cwd
  being a checked-out git repository
- project-scoped control roots separate config/state from runtime workspaces
  and remove implicit .git discovery from the service contract
- tests now encode the intended project-scoped behavior instead of relying
  on ambient session environment

Tests:
- make GO=/home/yuzhong/.local/go1.26.1/bin/go test
- LINT_BASE_REV=HEAD make GO=/home/yuzhong/.local/go1.26.1/bin/go lint

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(cockpit): add workflow family history comparison

Summary:
- add workflow-family comparison summaries and family-output reuse rows to the
  cockpit workflow inspector
- extend child, peer, and ancestor workflow rows with comparison metadata and
  direct output reuse actions
- update the implementation mirrors to record the shipped family-history surface

Rationale:
- Batch S made branch origin and best continuation visible, but operators still
  had to reconstruct how sibling or ancestor runs differed and which family
  output was worth reusing
- the roadmap still called out richer workflow history and comparison as a real
  cockpit gap after the first branch debugger shipped

Tests:
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx -t "surfaces workflow branch families and can continue the latest branch"
